example: The police went after the suspect immediately.
The new policy finally went through.
She picked up English quickly.
He fell over while playing football.
We need to think about how to go about solving this problem.
Many students go in for voluntary work.
She managed to put her ideas over clearly.
Old ideas may give way to new ones.
He refused to give in to pressure.
I plan to call on my teacher this weekend.
Anger eventually gave way to calm.
The crowd made for the exit.
His expression gave away his true feelings.

meaning: phr. 有发言权；有决定权
example: Students should have a say in school rules.
The plan is in line with our long-term goals.
She appears to be confident about the result.
Tourism brings in a lot of income.
His careless words gave rise to misunderstanding.
Young people should learn to break away from bad habits.
